definition: A finite executable field-state proxy used to represent the paper's abstract framework state inside code.
The self-reference operator; F = T[F] denotes recursive stability or fixed-point consistency, not empirical proof that nature realizes the model.
The successor-producing operator that maps a parent state F to F' under norm preservation, bounded similarity, and positive residual constraints.
A generated state in the same lineage as F that must be self-similar, non-colinear, and non-identical to the parent.
A recursive inheritance and scaling parameter constrained to 0 < alpha < 1 in executable operators.
The novelty coefficient in the W operator; it controls the strength of the bounded gradient contribution.
The embodiment or coupling-strength coefficient in the W operator; it scales the novelty term without changing the parent-norm target.
The phase-rotation parameter applied to the shifted parent state inside the W operator.
The normalized overlap |<F,F'>|/(norm(F) norm(F')) used to measure recognizable structure across parent and successor.
The normalized distance between parent and successor; it must remain positive so W does not collapse into cloning.
A deliberately limited computational representation of a broader physical claim; it constrains internal behavior without claiming empirical proof.
Agreement between paper statements, data ledgers, implementations, and passing tests inside the repository.
Independent measurement, observation, or accepted external data required before a physical mapping can be treated as empirically confirmed.
The claim ledger that maps each executable paper claim to its paper anchor, implementation path, pytest function, evidence type, and external validation boundary.
The formula ledger that maps displayed mathematical relations to executable code and associated tests.
The classification protocol separating executable claims, bounded proxies, formalization gaps, external empirical targets, context, and presentation risks.
The standing attack ledger that records known critique vectors and the repository evidence meant to answer or absorb them.
A failure mode where terms, formulas, or tests keep their wording while their meaning shifts across paper, README, data, or code.
